data.title: Sketches of Spain
data.label: Columbia Records
data.summary: A collaboration with arranger Gil Evans that dissolves the boundary between jazz and Spanish classical music.
data.liner_notes: Miles and Gil Evans's third and most ambitious collaboration, *Sketches of Spain* transposes Joaquín Rodrigo's *Concierto de Aranjuez* and Manuel de Falla's *El amor brujo* into a new language. Over 70 sessions spread across late 1959 and early 1960, Evans built orchestral tapestries around Miles's singing flugelhorn. The result is neither jazz nor classical — it exists in a space Miles carved out alone.
